boolean_fields.autoplay: Controls whether playback starts automatically as soon as the media is ready. Leave it disabled when playback should begin only after an explicit user action.
boolean_fields.controls: Controls whether the platform's native playback controls are shown, such as play/pause, seek, volume, or fullscreen actions when available.
boolean_fields.dismissible: Controls whether the overlay can be closed by outside interaction instead of requiring an explicit close action.
boolean_fields.enabled: Controls whether the user can interact with the control. Disable it to show the control without allowing input or activation.
boolean_fields.interactive: Controls whether hover, press, focus, or other interaction tracking is enabled for this control.
boolean_fields.loading: Controls whether the control should render its loading state instead of the normal idle state.
boolean_fields.loop: Controls whether playback restarts automatically after the media reaches the end of the stream.
boolean_fields.muted: Controls whether audio starts muted. The media can still render visually while sound remains off until changed by the runtime or user.
boolean_fields.open: Controls whether the overlay or transient surface is currently visible to the user.
boolean_fields.scrollable: Controls whether overflowing content is wrapped in a scrollable host instead of being laid out at its full intrinsic size.
boolean_fields.selectable: Controls whether rendered text content can be selected and copied by the user.

generic_fields.events: List of runtime event names that should be emitted back to Python for this control instance.
generic_fields.focus_ring: Focus-ring configuration applied when the control receives keyboard or accessibility focus. Typical values control ring color, width, inset, or animation depending on the renderer.
generic_fields.motion_behavior: Motion configuration used when transitioning between interaction states. Use it to tune duration, easing, and animation behavior for hover, press, focus, or selection changes.
generic_fields.props: Raw prop overrides merged into the payload sent to Flutter. Use this when the Python wrapper does not yet expose a runtime key as a first-class argument.
generic_fields.strict: Enables strict validation for unsupported or unknown props when schema checks are available. This is useful while developing wrappers or debugging payload mismatches.
generic_fields.style: Local style map merged into the rendered control payload. Use it for per-instance styling without changing shared tokens, variants, or recipe classes.

state_styles.base: Base style map applied in the idle state before any interactive or disabled overrides are merged.
state_styles.disabled: State-specific style map applied when the control is disabled. Use it to tone down interactive styling or replace it with a non-interactive appearance.
state_styles.hover: State-specific style map applied while the pointer hovers the control. Use it to override hover-time visual properties such as background, border, elevation, or text color.
state_styles.pressed: State-specific style map applied while the control is actively pressed. Use it for press-time feedback such as scale, elevation, tint, or shadow changes.

token_buckets.button: Mapping of named button style tokens to reusable button appearances consumed by recipes, variants, or control defaults.
token_buckets.card: Mapping of named card style tokens to reusable surface, border, and elevation settings for card-like components.
token_buckets.colors: Mapping of named color tokens to the concrete color values used by the renderer. Use this bucket to centralize reusable semantic or brand colors.
token_buckets.elevation: Mapping of named elevation tokens to reusable shadow, blur, or surface depth settings.
token_buckets.motion: Mapping of named motion tokens to reusable duration, easing, or transition specifications used by animated controls.
token_buckets.radius: Mapping of named radius tokens to the concrete corner-radius values used by the renderer. Use this bucket to centralize reusable radius tokens for themes, recipes, or component defaults.
token_buckets.spacing: Mapping of named spacing tokens to the gap, padding, or margin values consumed by the renderer.
token_buckets.typography: Mapping of named typography tokens to reusable text-style values such as size, weight, family, or line-height settings.
token_buckets.ui: Mapping of shared UI tokens used across general-purpose controls, surfaces, and layout primitives.
